entity.requestQueries.description: Query parameters for the HTTP request.

entity.requestBody.description: The body of the HTTP request.

entity.responseBody.description: The body of the HTTP response.

entity.requestHeader.description: A single form of the HTTP request header.

message.free.description: Indicates that the price is free, no points or currency are required to use the API service.

message.appliedCount.description: The number of times the API service has been applied for.

message.firstFree.description: Indicates that the first use of the API service is free, no points or currency are required.

message.custom.description: Indicates that the price is a specified amount, points or currency are required to use the API service.

message.present.description: We will gift points to you upon your first use of the API service.

message.around.description: The price is approximately, the actual price may vary.

message.successRate15min.description: The real-time success rate of the API service in the past 15 minutes.

message.ySuccessRate.description: The success rate of the API service.

message.streamUsage.message: Note: The above code may not fully apply to streaming requests. Please refer to the integration documentation for modifications.
message.streamUsage.description: The above code may not fully apply to streaming requests. Please refer to the integration documentation for modifications.

message.alphaStage.message: This API is in the Alpha stage, an early preview version that may have potential instability. The API format may undergo significant changes, and it is not recommended for production use. Current pricing is relatively low, and there may be significant price adjustments in the future.
message.alphaStage.description: This API is in the Alpha stage, an early preview version that may have potential instability. The API format may undergo significant changes, and it is not recommended for production use. Current pricing is relatively low, and there may be significant price adjustments in the future.

message.betaStage.message: This API is in the Beta stage, the API format is unlikely to change significantly, but there may be potential instability. Please use with caution in production environments. Current pricing is relatively low, and there may be moderate price adjustments in the future.
message.betaStage.description: This API is in the Beta stage, the API format is unlikely to change significantly, but there may be potential instability. Please use with caution in production environments. Current pricing is relatively low, and there may be moderate price adjustments in the future.

unit.credit.description: Credits are the virtual currency used in the API service.

unit.Credit.description: Credits are the virtual currency used in the API service, this is the uppercase form.

unit.credits.description: Credits are the virtual currency used in the API service, this is the plural form.

unit.Credits.description: Credits are the virtual currency used in the API service, this is the uppercase and plural form.

unit.call.description: A call represents the number of times the API service has been called.

unit.count.description: Times represents the number of times the API service has been called.

unit.Call.description: A call represents the number of times the API service has been called, this is the uppercase form.

unit.calls.description: Calls represent the number of times the API service has been called, this is the plural form.

unit.Calls.description: Calls represent the number of times the API service has been called, this is the uppercase and plural form.

unit.MB.description: MB stands for megabyte, a unit of digital information storage.

unit.GB.description: GB stands for gigabyte, a unit of digital information storage.

message.cheaperThan.description: Text displayed when the current interface price is lower than the comparison target, '$[[value]]' will be replaced with the target name at runtime.

message.moreExpensiveThan.description: Text displayed when the current interface price is higher than the comparison target, '$[[value]]' will be replaced with the target name at runtime.
